intro: Humularia minima, also known as the "Small Humularia," is a plant species in the family Leguminosae, native to tropical regions of Central and West Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are believed to aid in treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Humularia minima include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Humularia minima is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is often used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as to support immune function and reduce inflammation.
